title: Abel Tasman Water Taxi
meta_desc: Experience Abel Tasman National Park with kids on a water taxi, swim, explore, and enjoy world-class beaches, suitable for all ages, from $45/child, $70/adult
intro: The Abel Tasman Water Taxi & Beach Day is a fantastic way to explore Abel Tasman National Park with your family. With prices starting from $45 per child and $70 per adult, it's an affordable and fun day out. The water taxi service runs regularly from Kaiteriteri or Marahau, dropping you off at stunning beaches like Anchorage Bay or Bark Bay.

sections.heading: What to Expect
sections.body:
The day starts with a scenic water taxi ride to your chosen beach. Once you arrive, you're free to spend the day swimming, building sandcastles, and exploring the surrounding area. The beaches are truly world-class, with golden sand and crystal-clear waters.

We found the water taxi service to be efficient and reliable, with friendly staff who were happy to help with any questions or concerns. The beaches were clean and well-maintained, with basic facilities like toilets and picnic tables.

One of the best things about this experience is that it's suitable for all ages, from babies to grandparents. Our kids loved playing in the sand and swimming in the calm waters, while we enjoyed relaxing on the beach and taking in the stunning scenery.
list: World-class beaches with golden sand and crystal-clear waters
Basic facilities like toilets and picnic tables

sections.heading: Value for Money
sections.body:
We found the Abel Tasman Water Taxi & Beach Day to be great value for money, especially considering the quality of the experience. At $45 per child and $70 per adult, it's an affordable day out for families.

However, it's worth noting that you'll need to factor in the cost of food and drinks, as well as any additional activities you might want to do, such as kayaking or hiking.

sections.heading: Insider Tips and Best Time to Visit
sections.body:
To make the most of your day, we recommend booking your water taxi tickets in advance, especially during peak season (December to February). It's also a good idea to check the weather forecast and plan your day accordingly.

The best time to visit Abel Tasman National Park is during the summer months, when the weather is warm and sunny. However, if you're looking for a more relaxed experience, consider visiting during the shoulder season (March to May or September to November).
list: Book your water taxi tickets in advance
Visit during the summer months for warm weather

sections.heading: Booking and Saving Money
sections.body:
To book your Abel Tasman Water Taxi & Beach Day, simply visit the official website or contact the water taxi operator directly. You can also book through a local tour operator or travel agent.

To save money, consider booking a family pass or looking out for special deals and discounts. You can also pack your own food and drinks to avoid buying them at the beach.
